name: i18n
summary: Lean localization with a per-screen domain-merge architecture: a small base catalog + per-screen catalog modules merged on top, typed t() with interpolation, RTL, persisted locale — no heavy i18n library.
postInstall: Wrap the app in <I18nProvider>, then use const { t } = useTranslation() (or useLocale()).
Base strings (common/auth/greeting) live in src/locales/{en,fr}.ts — add a string to en first, fr is type-checked to match.

Add a SCREEN's strings: create src/locales/screens/<screen>.ts exporting defineScreenDomain({ en, fr }), then register it in src/locales/screens/index.ts (SCREEN_DOMAINS) and src/catalogs.ts (TranslationKey union). buildLocale() folds it on top of the base — two screens can be translated in parallel.
For RTL languages, add the code to RTL_LOCALES; applyDirectionForLocale() flips the layout (needs a reload to fully apply).
